subject: Databases
title: ACID vs BASE
description: A tiny lesson with a big payoff: ACID vs BASE.

hook.text: ACID guarantees strict correctness. BASE (Basically Available, Soft state, Eventually consistent) trades it for scale.

buildup.text: ACID databases (Postgres, MySQL) prioritize correctness. Every read reflects the latest committed write.

discovery.text: BASE systems (Cassandra, DynamoDB) prioritize availability and partition tolerance. Data converges eventually.

twist.text: The choice isn't binary. DynamoDB offers both eventual and strong consistency per query. Mix and match.

climax.text: Use ACID for money, inventory, and auth. Use BASE for feeds, analytics, and caches.

punchline.text: ACID for correctness. BASE for scale. Many apps need both.

quiz.question: What does the 'E' in BASE stand for?
options: Eventually consistent — data across replicas converges over time
Encrypted — all data is stored securely
Efficient — queries always run in constant time
